methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $87M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Osaro is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Derik Pridmore and Michael Kahane. The firm has raised $86.8M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private robotics firms at the same stage and 1 public comparable, providing reasonable reference points for valuation despite the nascent and heterogeneous nature of the sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $87M and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $165M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $140M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $620M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding stage-specific multiples in an emerging sector where comparable companies exhibit valuations varying by more than 30x. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer median is heavily influenced by outlier outliers, particularly 1X Technologies. Key uncertainties include revenue scale, gross margins, and whether frontier robotics firms are pricing for near-term deployment or long-dated optionality.
